tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: After clearing the sentinel, upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc exited RC=1. Functional upgrade portion succeeded (poc_mode now sponsored_poc, files updated, audit-entry committed) but the post-upgrade 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' block triggered macOS cp self-copy error: 'cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and <project>/scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied)'. With set -euo pipefail this aborts the script. NOTE: this is NOT U-E regression — poc_mode lookup worked correctly ('Current POC mode: private poc').

tests.details: .current_phase still 2; .project still 'uat-agent-70'

tests.name: sponsored_poc_state_reflected
tests.details: .poc_mode = 'sponsored_poc' after upgrade; CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_INTAKE.md/APPROVAL_LOG.md updated; chore(upgrade) commit created

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh exits 1 due to cp self-copy in helper-refresh block
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a project whose scripts/upgrade-project.sh is identical to $FRAMEWORK/scripts/upgrade-project.sh (i.e., any project in this UAT regime where init.sh just copied helper scripts), run: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc
framework_bugs.expected: RC=0; the BL-009/BL-015 helper refresh either no-ops cleanly when source==destination, or skips when the project script is the running script
framework_bugs.actual: RC=1. The block at scripts/upgrade-project.sh:1469-1480 runs `cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper"` where SCRIPT_DIR is derived from BASH_SOURCE — when invoked as `bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh ...` from the project, SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ directory, so the cp source == destination. macOS BSD cp errors with 'are identical (not copied)' and returns nonzero, and `set -euo pipefail` aborts. Functional upgrade (poc_mode bump, file rewrites, commit) had already completed so the project IS upgraded, but the script reports failure and skips the final post-upgrade validation step. Linux GNU cp may behave differently (silent no-op) — bug is at minimum macOS-specific. Suggest comparing realpath of source vs destination before cp, or using `cp -f` with stderr suppression for known-identical case, or scoping SCRIPT_DIR to ORCHESTRATOR_ROOT explicitly.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh prints no new-requirement guidance for private_poc → sponsored_poc
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run any upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc with deployment already organizational and no track change
framework_bugs.expected: Output includes 'Next steps for sponsored POC:' enumerating new artifacts/docs the user must produce (e.g., sponsor sign-off, governance section reviews, updated APPROVAL_LOG sponsor approver fields)
framework_bugs.actual: Only the per-condition blocks (deployment-changed, track-changed, poc-removed) print next-step guidance. private_poc → sponsored_poc never enters any of those branches, so the user gets only the 'Upgrade Complete' banner with no instructions about what additional artifacts the sponsored POC mode requires.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook test 'new_required_artifacts_listed' assumes upgrade prints sponsored-poc-specific guidance, but no such block exists in upgrade-project.sh

Framework integrity preserved.

summary: Init/CDF/baseline/Phase 1/Phase 2 all worked; sentinel-block fix verified; but upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc exits RC=1 on macOS due to a cp self-copy in the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block (source==destination because SCRIPT_DIR resolves to the project's own scripts/ when the script is invoked as bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh). Functional upgrade still completed (poc_mode now sponsored_poc), but the script's reported failure plus missing private→sponsored next-step guidance and U-D confirmation drag overall_pass to false.
